name: First Principles Thinking
what_it_is: Breaking a problem to its most fundamental truths and rebuilding from there - rather than reasoning by analogy from existing solutions.
core_mechanic: Ask: "What do we know for certain is true?" Strip assumptions, conventions, and analogies until only verified truths remain. Ask: "What can we build from these truths?"
when_to_use: Existing solutions are hitting a ceiling / Inherited a problem framing that might itself be wrong / Competitive differentiation requires something fundamentally different / A constraint you're designing around might not be real
when_not_to_use: Analogical reasoning is sufficient and speed matters / Operational problems / Domain fundamentals are genuinely uncertain
ideaos_application: Agent 3 identifies 3 core assumptions in the problem statement, challenges each with "is this true or just convention?", proposes one direction only visible after removing the most constraining assumption.
